error_handling.overview: Common error scenarios in frontend design work and how to recover from them.

error.problem: Classes not applying or appearing incorrectly

causes.cause: Missing cn() wrapper causing class conflicts
Tailwind class not included in the build (dynamic class construction)
CSS variable not defined in globals.css

error.recovery: 1. Verify the class is a valid Tailwind utility or custom class 2. Ensure cn() is used for conditional merging 3. Check that dynamic classes use full class names (not string concatenation) 4. Confirm the CSS variable exists in both :root and .dark

1. Check for hardcoded widths (w-[Npx]) that should be responsive 2. Add appropriate breakpoint prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:) 3. Use min-w-0 on flex children to prevent overflow 4. Verify grid template columns adapt across breakpoints

error.problem: Component looks broken in dark mode

causes.cause: CSS variable not updated in .dark selector

error.recovery: 1. Replace hardcoded colors with semantic tokens (bg-background, text-foreground) 2. Add dark: overrides where tokens don't cover the use case 3. Verify the CSS variable is defined in the .dark block of globals.css

error.problem: New component doesn't match existing design language

causes.cause: Not following the cva + cn() pattern
Using different border-radius, shadow, or spacing values

error.recovery: 1. Compare with an existing similar component for pattern alignment 2. Use the same radius tokens (rounded-md, rounded-lg) 3. Apply consistent shadow utilities (shadow-xs, shadow-sm) 4. Add data-slot attribute matching the component name

prevention.practice: Always read an existing similar component before creating a new one
Use design tokens exclusively — never hardcode colors or spacing
Test dark mode mentally for every style change
Verify responsive behavior at all breakpoints
